data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix utilizes a microservices architecture to handle its vast operational needs, allowing independent deployment and scalability of individual services.
data.technical_spec: Deployed on AWS with services running in Docker containers managed by Kubernetes.

data.title: Distributed Computing
data.description: Netflix leverages distributed computing for content delivery, enabling efficient streaming processes and data handling across the globe.
data.technical_spec: Apache Cassandra for NoSQL database needs, with AWS S3 for file storage.

data.title: Global CDN
data.description: Netflix uses its own content delivery network, Open Connect, to improve streaming efficiency and reduce latency.
data.technical_spec: Data centers located worldwide with peering partnerships to ISPs.

data.title: Monolithic Content Platform
data.description: A robust platform for content discovery and recommendations integrated into the main app ecosystem.
data.technical_spec: Powered by algorithms in a centralized service using AWS Lambda for serverless computing.

data.title: Edge Computing
data.description: Netflix uses edge computing to deliver content closer to the end-user and improve overall service quality.
data.technical_spec: Implemented through AWS Wavelength and direct peering, lowering latency for streaming.
